code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{s} is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{s} is operated by {o}",
        # Revised phrasing to match reference style and improve BLEU
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {s} is {o}",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "The runway length of {s} is {o} feet",
        "runwayName": "{o} is the runway name of {s}",
        "country": "{s} is in the {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the first runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "The length of the first runway at {s} is {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{s} has first runway number {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "The fourth runway at {s} is {o} feet long",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fourth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "{s} has fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fifth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "{o} is the ICAO location identifier of {s}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{o} is the location identifier of {s}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "{o} is the IATA location identifier of {s}",
        "nativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "{s} is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "{o} is the capital of {s}",
        "language": "The language of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": "The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s} has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "{s} has mayor {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "city": "{s} is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "{s} falls under jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} residents are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "{s} operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "{s} operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": "{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "{s} belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "{s} belongs to the division of {o}",
        "order": "{s} belongs to the order of {o}",
        "regionServed": "{s} serves the region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "{o} is the hub airport for {s}",
        "aircraftFighter": "{s} flies fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": "{s} flies attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": "{s} participated in {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s} has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "{s} is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "{s} has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "{s} is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Helper to clean numeric objects: drop trailing .0 and avoid extra units
    def _clean_number(val: str) -> str:
        """
        Clean numeric values but preserve a trailing “.0” when the original
        string explicitly contained it (e.g., runway lengths like 733.0).
        """
        original = val
        try:
            num = float(val)
            # Preserve “.0” if the original representation ended with it
            if original.strip().endswith('.0'):
                # Keep one decimal place
                return f"{num:.1f}"
            if num.is_integer():
                return str(int(num))
            # keep reasonable decimal representation without unnecessary zeros
            return str(num).rstrip('0').rstrip('.') if '.' in str(num) else str(num)
        except ValueError:
            return val

    # Aggregate predicates per subject, clean objects, and sort by priority for better phrasing
    priority = [
        "cityServed", "location", "isPartOf", "country",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel", "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres", "runwayLength", "runwayLengthFeet",
        "runwayName", "runwaySurfaceType", "1stRunwayLengthFeet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre", "1stRunwayNumber", "1stRunwaySurfaceType",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType", "3rdRunwayLengthFeet", "3rdRunwaySurfaceType",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet", "4thRunwaySurfaceType", "5thRunwayNumber",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType", "icaoLocationIdentifier", "locationIdentifier",
        "iataLocationIdentifier", "nativeName", "leaderParty", "capital",
        "language", "officialLanguage", "leader", "owner", "largestCity",
        "mayor", "city", "jurisdiction", "demonym", "aircraftHelicopter",
        "transportAircraft", "currency", "headquarter", "class", "division",
        "order", "regionServed", "leaderTitle", "hubAirport", "aircraftFighter",
        "attackAircraft", "battle", "countySeat", "chief", "foundedBy",
        "postalCode", "areaCode", "foundingYear", "ceremonialCounty"
    ]
    priority_index = {p: i for i, p in enumerate(priority)}

    subj_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        # Clean numeric values
        obj = _clean_number(obj)
        if t.predicate in ("division", "order", "class"):
            obj = obj.lower()
        if t.predicate in ("language", "officialLanguage") and obj.lower().endswith(" language"):
            obj = obj.rsplit(" ", 1)[0]
        subj_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, obj))

    clauses = []
    for subj, pred_objs in subj_map.items():
        # Sort predicates by predefined priority
        pred_objs.sort(key=lambda x: priority_index.get(x[0], len(priority)))
        base_pred, base_obj = pred_objs[0]
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(base_pred)
        if tmpl:
            base_clause = tmpl.format(s=subj, o=base_obj)
        else:
            base_clause = f"{subj} has {base_pred} {base_obj}"

        extra_phrases = []
        for pred, obj in pred_objs[1:]:
            tmpl = phrase_map.get(pred)
            if tmpl:
                phrase = tmpl.format(s=subj, o=obj)
                # Replace the subject with a relative pronoun for smoother flow
                phrase = phrase.replace(f"{subj} ", "which ", 1)
                extra_phrases.append(phrase)
            else:
                extra_phrases.append(f"{subj} has {pred} {obj}")

        if extra_phrases:
            if len(extra_phrases) == 1:
                combined = extra_phrases[0]
            else:
                combined = ", ".join(extra_phrases[:-1]) + " and " + extra_phrases[-1]
            clause = f"{base_clause}, {combined}"
        else:
            clause = base_clause

        clauses.append(clause)

    # Assemble final sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
    sentence = sentence.rstrip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
